have h₁ : Reversible x y p := h
    exfalso
    -- If y is reversible in x, then we need to derive a contradiction.
    -- Since h : Reversible x y p, we know that y is reversible in x.
    -- We will use this fact to show that z cannot be reversible in x.unreverse.
    cases p with
    | left =>
      -- Case p = left
      have h₂ : ∃ (z : IGame), z ∈ y.rightMoves ∧ z ≤ x := by
        simpa [reversible_left] using h₁
      obtain ⟨w, hw₁, hw₂⟩ := h₂
      -- Since y is reversible in x, there exists a right move w of y such that w ≤ x.
      -- We will use this fact to show that z cannot be reversible in x.unreverse.
      have h₃ : x.unreverse.leftMoves = ⋃ (y : x.leftMoves), reverseSet x y left |>.image unreverse := by
        simp [moves_unreverse]
      -- Simplify the left moves of x.unreverse.
      have h₄ : x.unreverse.rightMoves = ⋃ (y : x.rightMoves), reverseSet x y right |>.image unreverse := by
        simp [moves_unreverse]
      -- Simplify the right moves of x.unreverse.
      simp_all [reversible_left]
      <;>
      (try contradiction) <;>
      (try aesop) <;>
      (try simp_all [reverseSet, moves_unreverse]) <;>
      (try norm_num) <;>
      (try omega)
    | right =>
      -- Case p = right
      have h₂ : ∃ (z : IGame), z ∈ y.leftMoves ∧ x ≤ z := by
        simpa [reversible_right] using h₁
      obtain ⟨w, hw₁, hw₂⟩ := h₂
      -- Since y is reversible in x, there exists a left move w of y such that x ≤ w.
      -- We will use this fact to show that z cannot be reversible in x.unreverse.
      have h₃ : x.unreverse.rightMoves = ⋃ (y : x.rightMoves), reverseSet x y right |>.image unreverse := by
        simp [moves_unreverse]
      -- Simplify the right moves of x.unreverse.
      have h₄ : x.unreverse.leftMoves = ⋃ (y : x.leftMoves), reverseSet x y left |>.image unreverse := by
        simp [moves_unreverse]
      -- Simplify the left moves of x.unreverse.
      simp_all [reversible_right]
      <;>
      (try contradiction) <;>
      (try aesop) <;>
      (try simp_all [reverseSet, moves_unreverse]) <;>
      (try norm_num) <;>
      (try omega)
  · -- Case: y is not reversible in x.
    exfalso
    -- Since h : ¬Reversible x y p, we know that y is not reversible in x.

        have h₅₂ : Subposition z x := h₄
        have h₅₃ : (unreverse z).birthday ≤ z.birthday := by
          apply birthday_unreverse_le
        have h₅₄ : z.birthday < x.birthday := Subposition.birthday_lt h₅₂
        linarith
      have h₅₅ : x.birthday ≤ (x.unreverse).birthday := by
        have h₅₆ : x.unreverse ≈ x := unreverse_equiv x
        have h₅₇ : x.birthday = (x.unreverse).birthday := by
          rw [eq_comm]

            apply Eq.symm
            apply Eq.symm
            rw [← IGame.birthday_congr h₅₆]
            <;> rfl
        linarith
      linarith
    -- Use the fact that the birthday of `unreverse z` is less than the birthday of `x.unreverse` to show a contradiction
    have h₆ : ¬ Reversible x.unreverse (unreverse z) p := by
      intro h₆
      have h₇ : (unreverse z).birthday < (x.unreverse).birthday := h₅
      have h₈ : (unreverse z).birthday ≥ (x.unreverse).birthday := by
        -- Use the reversibility to show a contradiction with the birthday
        have h₉ := h₆.choose_spec
        cases p with
        | left =>
          -- For the left player, use the fact that `h.choose ∈ (unreverse z).R`

            simpa [reverseSet_of_reversible] using h₆.choose_spec.1
          have h₁₁ : Subposition h₆.choose (unreverse z) := Subposition.of_mem_moves_right h₁₀
          have h₁₂ : (h₆.choose).birthday < (unreverse z).birthday := Subposition.birthday_lt h₁₁
          have h₁₃ : (h₆.choose).birthday ≤ (x.unreverse).birthday := by
            have h₁₄ : h₆.choose ≤ x.unreverse := by
              have h₁₅ : h₆.choose ≤ x.unreverse := by
                -- Use the fact that `h.choose ≤ x.unreverse`

                exact h₁₆
              exact h₁₅
            have h₁₅ : (h₆.choose).birthday ≤ (x.unreverse).birthday := by
              apply birthday_le_of_le h₁₄
            exact h₁₅
          linarith
        | right =>
          -- For the right player, use the fact that `h.choose ∈ (unreverse z).L`
          have h₁₀ : h₆.choose ∈ (unreverse z).L := by
            simpa [reverseSet_of_reversible] using h₆.choose_spec.1
          have h₁₁ : Subposition h₆.choose (unreverse z) := Subposition.of_mem_moves_left h₁₀
          have h₁₂ : (h₆.choose).birthday < (unreverse z).birthday := Subposition.birthday_lt h₁₁
          have h₁₃ : (x.unreverse).birthday ≤ (h₆.choose).birthday := by
            have h₁₄ : x.unreverse ≤ h₆.choose := by
              have h₁₅ : x.unreverse ≤ h₆.choose := by
